It also discusses tariff choice by using the Victorian Default Offer rate and noting off-peak vs peak impacts, enabling evaluation of differing TCO calculations.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response explicitly explains that the Australian Energy Rating Label uses 52 cycles/year (one load per week) and then scales the labeled annual kWh to a 6–8 loads/week household by converting to per-cycle kWh and multiplying by ~364 cycles/year (7 loads/week). This directly demonstrates the realistic usage comparison requested.

citation-quality.requirement: References Heritage Overlay regulations or heritage permit requirements for Melbourne properties
